name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax offers vehicle title loans and title pawns, letting customers borrow cash using a paid-off car or motorcycle title as collateral while keeping their vehicle.
description_long:
TitleMax opened its first store on September 1, 1998 in Columbus, Georgia, and has grown into one of the largest vehicle title lenders in the United States. The company is headquartered at 15 Bull Street in Savannah, Georgia, and operates as a subsidiary of TMX Finance LLC, owned by Tracy Young. After filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in April 2009, TitleMax emerged from restructuring in April 2010 and has since expanded to 900+ stores across 14+ states. TitleMax holds no CDFI certification, HUD approval, or NFCC membership — it is a for-profit, high-cost consumer lender, not a nonprofit or community development institution.

TitleMax's core product is the vehicle title loan: customers bring a paid-off car or motorcycle, hand over the title as collateral, and walk out with cash — often in as little as 30 minutes — while keeping and driving their vehicle throughout the repayment period. Loan amounts reach up to $10,000 for standard customers and up to $25,000 for qualified borrowers in select markets. In Georgia, the same service is structured as a title pawn. TitleMax also offers unsecured personal installment loans up to $1,500 (first-time caps vary: $600 in Mississippi, $800 in South Carolina, $1,000 in other markets) — these require a bank account and proof of income. No credit check is required for title loan or pawn products. The company operates in Alabama, Arizona, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Mississippi, Missouri, Nevada, New Mexico, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, and Wisconsin, with an online presence in Idaho.

TitleMax's key differentiators are speed, accessibility, and physical scale. The 30-minute in-store funding process and no-credit-check approval make it one of the fastest collateral lenders available to credit-challenged borrowers. Its 900+ store footprint is among the largest of any title lender in the country. Borrowers are not required to surrender their vehicle, which distinguishes title lending from traditional pawn. A customer portal accessible via mobile browser lets borrowers check balances, review due dates, and make payments at any hour. There are no prepayment penalties, so borrowers can reduce their total interest cost by paying off early.

The honest limitations of TitleMax are significant. Third-party reviewers consistently report APRs ranging from 100% to over 300%, and monthly fees can reach 25% of the borrowed amount depending on state law — exact rates are not published on the website and require a formal application to obtain. Defaulting on the loan risks vehicle repossession, which can have serious consequences for borrowers who rely on their car for work. TitleMax is not BBB accredited, and complaint volumes on the BBB and ComplaintsBoard.com (rated 2.0/5 from ~87 complaints) reflect recurring issues with hidden fees, balances not decreasing despite payments, and repossession disputes. This product is best viewed as a last-resort option when lower-cost alternatives are unavailable.
address: 1337 E Parkerville Rd Ste B, DeSoto, TX 75115

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee. TitleMax issues binding loan contracts secured by a vehicle title. Failure to repay may result in vehicle repossession.

pros: No credit check required — borrowers with poor or no credit history can qualify for title loans
No bank account required for car or motorcycle title loans
In-store funding in as little as 30 minutes from application to cash
Borrowers keep and drive their vehicle throughout the entire repayment period
900+ store locations across 14+ states provide broad physical access
No prepayment penalty — paying off early reduces total interest owed
24/7 online customer portal for viewing balances, due dates, and making payments
cons: APRs typically range from 100% to over 300% — far above any bank or credit union product
Monthly fees can reach 25% of the borrowed amount depending on state, making rollovers extremely costly
Vehicle repossession is a real consequence of default, risking loss of transportation
Interest rates and fee schedules are not publicly disclosed — exact terms require a formal application
Not BBB accredited; recurring complaints on BBB and ComplaintsBoard.com cite hidden fees, balances not declining, and repossession disputes
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit history who need cash urgently and own a paid-off car or motorcycle
Borrowers who need funds in under an hour and cannot qualify for traditional bank loans or credit cards
Consumers who can repay the full loan quickly to minimize high-interest accumulation
People in the 14+ states TitleMax serves who lack nearby access to credit unions or lower-cost emergency lenders
diagnosis: TitleMax is genuinely best suited for vehicle-owning consumers facing an urgent cash shortfall who have exhausted lower-cost options — its no-credit-check title loans fund in 30 minutes with no bank account required. The critical caveat is cost: APRs of 100–300% and monthly fees up to 25% of the loan amount make this a high-risk last-resort product, and any borrower who cannot repay on schedule faces potential loss of their vehicle.
services: Car title loans (up to $10,000; up to $25,000 for qualified customers in select markets)
Motorcycle title loans (secured by motorcycle title, borrower keeps the bike)
Title pawns (Georgia stores — functionally equivalent to title loans)
Unsecured personal installment loans (up to $1,500; first-time max varies by state)
No-credit-check loan approval for title loan and pawn products
In-store loan origination with funding in as little as 30 minutes
Online loan application (available in select states)
Early payoff option with no prepayment penalty
24/7 customer portal — view account balance, due dates, and payment history
Online payment processing via customer portal
